audioAssets.name: Listening — New Team, Tight Deadline (US)
audioAssets.transcript: Maya joined Brightline Solutions two weeks ago as a junior analyst. On her very first project, the team was asked to migrate a client's data to a new system in just five days, a task that usually takes two weeks. At first, Maya felt overwhelmed. Instead of panicking, her team lead David divided the work into small daily targets and asked everyone to share their progress each morning. Maya was responsible for checking the data for errors. On the third day, she noticed that hundreds of customer records had duplicate entries. She immediately flagged the issue, and the team fixed it before it reached the client. In the end, they finished the migration a full day early. Maya learned that staying calm, asking questions, and communicating clearly matter just as much as technical skill. Her manager praised the whole team for their teamwork and attention to detail.

questions.title: Aptitude Q1
questions.body: A shopkeeper marks an item 25% above cost price and gives a 10% discount on the marked price. What is his profit percentage?

questions.title: Aptitude Q2
questions.body: If 15 workers can complete a task in 12 days, how many days will 20 workers take to complete the same task?

questions.body: A train 150 m long crosses a pole in 15 seconds. Find its speed in km/hr.

questions.body: The average of 5 numbers is 20. If one number is excluded, the average of the remaining 4 becomes 18. Find the excluded number.

test.title: Freshers Assessment 1
test.instructions:
Welcome to the Freshers Assessment. Please read carefully before you begin.

• Duration: 40 minutes — Aptitude (20 min) and Communication (20 min). Each section is timed separately and moves on automatically when its time ends.
• This is a proctored, lockdown assessment (Safe Exam Browser). Keep your webcam on, sit in a quiet, well-lit room, and do not switch windows or tabs.
• For spoken and written sections, use complete sentences and explain your point. We assess clarity, vocabulary, grammar, and how well you communicate — not your accent. Indian English is perfectly fine.
• You cannot return to a section once its timer ends. Attempt every question.

All the best!

sections.title: Aptitude
sections.description: 20 minutes. You will get 20 questions (from a larger pool) covering quantitative ability, logical reasoning, and data interpretation. 1 mark each. Choose the best option.

sections.title: Listen & Answer
sections.description: Listen to the audio (you may play it up to 2 times), then record a spoken answer to each of the 5 questions. Speak in full sentences and explain — we assess clarity and understanding, not accent.

sections.title: General English
sections.description: 10 grammar questions (tenses and prepositions). 1 mark each. Choose the correct option.

sections.title: JAM — Just A Minute
sections.description: You will get one topic. Think for a few seconds, then speak for about a minute in full sentences with examples.

sections.title: Writing
sections.description: Write about 50 words on the given prompt. Focus on clear, correct, well-organized English.
